articles.title: Suber hits 'miracle shot' for eagle to move top of leaderboard
articles.summary: American Jackson Suber sinks an eagle on the 17th hole to move to the top of the leaderboard on five under par in the first round of the Open Championship at Royal Birkdale Golf Club.

articles.title: Mary Jo, mother of Kardashian matriarch Kris Jenner, dies aged 91
articles.summary: Mary Jo Campbell was featured on multiple episodes of Keeping Up with the Kardashians over the years.

articles.title: 'I can't believe he did that' - best shots from first round at The Open
articles.summary: Enjoy the best shots from the first round of the 2026 Open Championship, featuring Scottie Scheffler and Rory McIlroy.

articles.title: Some England players disappointed at Tuchel's tactics
articles.summary: BBC Sport understands that Thomas Tuchel's defensive tactics in the World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina are being hotly debated by the England squad.

articles.title: Ebola spreading more quickly in DRC, while Uganda is close to being virus-free
articles.summary: Uganda ⁠⁠discharges last-remaining patient, as WHO says Ebola has 'expanded faster than any previous outbreak' in DRC.

articles.title: White House teleprompter operator accused of making $100k off Trump speech bets
articles.summary: A White House staffer has been accused of using inside knowledge of speeches to make nearly $100,000 on Kalshi.

articles.title: What could a US ground invasion of Iran look like?
articles.summary: Donald Trump has refused to rule out sending US troops into Iran as fighting resumes and US strikes continue.

articles.title: Fifa 'assessing reports' over Falklands banner
articles.summary: Fifa is "assessing the match reports" before deciding whether to punish Argentina after players held a banner in support of their country's claims to the Falkland Islands.

articles.title: Iran condemns ‘barbaric’ US attack near children’s cancer hospital
articles.summary: Iran accuses US of 'cowardly war crime' as doctor says blast was 'so close' they thought the hospital was struck.

articles.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
articles.summary: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

articles.title: Forest set to make £45m offer for Spurs' Bergvall
articles.summary: Nottingham Forest are set to make an offer of £45m for Tottenham midfielder Lucas Bergvall.

articles.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
articles.summary: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

articles.title: WHO: Ebola spreading faster than ever recorded in DR Congo
articles.summary: The World Health Organization warns the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo is spreading faster than any previous outbreak.

articles.title: From Southgate to Tuchel - has anything changed?
articles.summary: Thomas Tuchel was meant to be the man whose fresh approach would win England major honours, but defeat by Argentina felt like a familiar story - has anything really changed from the Gareth Southgate era?

articles.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
articles.summary: The same area devastated by floods last year - causing more than 130 deaths, including those at Camp Mystic - is once again being hit.

articles.title: In Syria’s Jobar, locals struggle to rebuild their destroyed homes
articles.summary: Jobar was held by rebels for much of Syria's 13-year war but was destroyed by repeated government assaults.

articles.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
articles.summary: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

articles.title: Canada wildfires spread smoke across North America, sparking air quality alerts
articles.summary: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has prompted air quality concerns in Toronto, New York and large parts of Michigan, Minneapolis and Minnesota.

articles.title: 'Goodness me, what a mess' - Brook out after 'horrible dismissal'
articles.summary: England captain Harry Brook is caught for 16 after playing a "horrible" shot while attempting a ramp to leave his side 53-3 chasing 234 to win against India in the second one-day international in Cardiff.

articles.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
articles.summary: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

articles.title: US approves nearly $2bn in weapons sale to Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: The sale intends to bolster Saudi air defence systems as the kingdom risks escalation with the Iran-backed Houthis.

articles.title: Investigation into parking tickets for drivers queuing at petrol stations
articles.summary: It's part of a wider crackdown from the consumer regulator into potentially unfair practices by private parking operators.

articles.title: 'Moment of joy' as Uganda discharges last Ebola patient

articles.title: Rare protests in Kyiv over dismissal of defence minister

articles.title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint

articles.title: Newsletter platform Beehiiv now lets subscribers chat with each other, adds AI

articles.title: 'Pressure on England' - India remove both openers for perfect start

articles.title: US to impose new 25 percent tariffs on some Brazilian imports

articles.title: Trump limits length of visas for students, exchange visitors, journalists

articles.title: Hamas leadership run-off expected between Meshaal and al-Hayya

articles.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line

articles.title: UK cops say arrest of two young hackers disrupted the operations of an infamous hacking group

articles.title: 'Excellent' Archer takes quick wickets to give England control

articles.title: Period tracker Stardust shares users’ health data with analytics firm, says Mozilla research
articles.summary: One period tracker app tested by Mozilla was 'squeaky clean,' while another app was seen sharing users' health data with an analytics company, underscoring vast differences in user privacy among these apps.
